question: [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take, fee-on-transfer token] Can a taker calling take() be blocked or cause accounting corruption when the loan token charges a fee on transfer, causing Midnight to receive less than buyerAssets - sellerAssets but accounting records the full amount, violating the invariant that ERC20 transfer deltas must match accounting deltas and that claimableSettlementFee must reflect actual received tokens? Proof idea: 1% fee-on-transfer MockERC20, call take(), assert claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] exceeds actual received tokens, leading to insolvency.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / repay, fee-on-transfer token] Can a borrower calling repay() be blocked or cause accounting corruption when the loan token charges a fee on transfer, causing Midnight to receive less than units but marketState.withdrawable is incremented by the full units amount, violating the invariant that withdrawable must not exceed actual token balance and that lenders must not be able to withdraw more than what was repaid? Proof idea: fee-on-transfer MockERC20, call repay(units=1000), assert withdrawable += 1000 but only 990 tokens received.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / supplyCollateral, fee-on-transfer token] Can a borrower calling supplyCollateral() be blocked or cause accounting corruption when the collateral token charges a fee on transfer, causing Midnight to record position.collateral[i] += assets but receive fewer tokens, violating the invariant that collateral accounting must match actual token balances and that health checks based on recorded collateral may overestimate actual collateral value? Proof idea: fee-on-transfer collateral token, call supplyCollateral(assets=1000), assert position.collateral[i] = 1000 but Midnight holds only 990 tokens.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdraw, rebasing token] Can a lender calling withdraw() be blocked when the loan token is a rebasing token whose balance decreases (negative rebase), causing Midnight's actual token balance to fall below marketState.withdrawable, so that safeTransfer reverts with an insufficient balance error from the token, violating the invariant that a lender with valid credit and withdrawable units must always be able to exit? Proof idea: negative-rebasing MockERC20, trigger rebase to reduce Midnight balance below withdrawable, call withdraw(), assert revert.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdrawCollateral, max-transfer-amount token] Can a borrower calling withdrawCollateral() be blocked when the collateral token enforces a maximum single-transfer amount (e.g., maxTransfer = 1000 tokens), causing safeTransfer to revert when assets > maxTransfer, even though the borrower has a healthy position and valid collateral balance, violating the invariant that a healthy borrower must always be able to withdraw collateral? Proof idea: MockCollateral with maxTransfer limit, call withdrawCollateral(assets > maxTransfer), assert revert blocks valid exit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdraw, max-transfer-amount token] Can a lender calling withdraw() be blocked when the loan token enforces a maximum single-transfer amount, causing safeTransfer to revert when units > maxTransfer, even though the lender has valid credit and the market has sufficient withdrawable balance, violating the invariant that a lender must always be able to exit? Proof idea: MockERC20 with maxTransfer limit, call withdraw(units > maxTransfer), assert revert.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / liquidate, collateral token reverts on transfer to address(0)] Can a liquidator calling liquidate() with receiver=address(0) be blocked when the collateral token reverts on transfer to address(0), even though the protocol does not validate receiver != address(0), violating the invariant that a valid liquidation of an unhealthy position must not be blocked by receiver address restrictions? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdraw, token returns 64 bytes with first 32 bytes = 0] Can a lender calling withdraw() be blocked when the loan token's transfer() returns 64 bytes where the first 32 bytes encode uint256(0), causing abi.decode(returndata, (bool)) to decode as false and trigger TransferReturnedFalse, even though the transfer was successful (some tokens return (bool success, uint256 amount) tuples), violating the invariant that a lender with valid credit must always be able to exit? Proof idea: MockERC20 returning abi.encode(false, uint256(1000)) from transfer, call withdraw(), assert TransferReturnedFalse.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take, token returns 64 bytes with first 32 bytes = 0] Can a taker calling take() be blocked when the loan token's transferFrom() returns 64 bytes where the first 32 bytes encode uint256(0), causing abi.decode(returndata, (bool)) to decode as false and trigger TransferFromReturnedFalse, even though the transfer was successful, violating the invariant that a valid offer fill must not be blocked by non-standard token return encoding? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take, token returns 64 bytes with first 32 bytes > 1] Can a taker calling take() be blocked when the loan token's transferFrom() returns 64 bytes where the first 32 bytes encode uint256(2), causing Solidity 0.8.x's strict bool ABI decoder to revert with a decoding error, even though the transfer was successful, violating the invariant that a valid offer fill must not be blocked by non-standard token return encoding? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdraw, multicall context] Can a lender using multicall() to batch withdraw() calls be blocked when the loan token's transfer() reverts on the second call (e.g., due to a per-block transfer limit), causing the entire multicall to revert and rolling back the first successful withdrawal, violating the invariant that multicall must not leave partial state and that valid individual operations must not be blocked by token-level per-call restrictions? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take, multicall context] Can a taker using multicall() to batch take() calls be blocked when the loan token's transferFrom() reverts on the second take (e.g., due to a per-transaction transfer limit), causing the entire multicall to revert and rolling back the first take's state updates (consumed, credit, debt), violating the invariant that multicall must not leave partial state and that consumed amounts must not be incremented without completing the transfer? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take, oracle-dependent token] Can a taker calling take() be blocked when the loan token's transferFrom() internally calls an oracle and reverts if the oracle returns an unexpected value (e.g., a token that checks a price oracle before allowing transfers), causing safeTransferFrom to revert after all position state has been updated, violating the invariant that take() must not be blocked by token-internal oracle dependencies that are independent of Midnight's oracle? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take, token with allowance race condition] Can a taker calling take() be blocked when the loan token implements USDT-style allowance semantics (requires approve(0) before approve(N) when current allowance > 0), causing transferFrom to revert when the payer's current allowance is non-zero but less than buyerAssets, even though the payer intended to set a new allowance, violating the invariant that a valid offer fill with sufficient payer balance must not be blocked by token allowance mechanics? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / liquidate, collateral token with transfer cooldown] Can a liquidator calling liquidate() be blocked when the collateral token enforces a cooldown period between transfers (e.g., last transfer was too recent), causing safeTransfer to revert even though the position is unhealthy and the liquidation is valid, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must always remain liquidatable and that token-level transfer restrictions must not block liquidation? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdraw, token reverts on transfer when sender has insufficient balance] Can a lender calling withdraw() be blocked when Midnight's actual token balance is less than units due to a previous fee-on-transfer or rebasing event, causing the loan token's transfer() to revert with insufficient balance, even though marketState.withdrawable >= units, violating the invariant that withdrawable must always be backed by actual token balance?
